---
pagetitle: "Experience"
---

```{=html}
<nav class="nav">
  <div class="nav-inner">
    <a href="index.html" class="brand">EJ.</a>
    <ul class="nav-links">
      <li><a href="about.html">About</a></li>
      <li><a href="experience.html" class="active">Experience</a></li>
      <li><a href="projects.html">Projects</a></li>
      <li><a href="hobbies.html">Hobbies</a></li>
      <li><a href="insights.html">Insights</a></li>
    </ul>
    <a href="contact.html" class="btn-dark">Get in touch</a>
  </div>
</nav>

<main>
<div class="page reveal">
  <div class="eyebrow page-eyebrow">Experience</div>
  <h1 class="page-title">A working timeline.</h1>

  <div class="timeline">
    <div class="tl-item">
      <div class="tl-when">Mar 2026 — Jun 2026</div>
      <div class="tl-title">AI Engineer</div>
      <div class="tl-org">Dhauz | QuantumRise — San Diego, CA</div>
      <div class="tl-desc">
        In my MSBA capstone with Dhauz AI Consulting and QuantumRise, I work at the intersection
        of business requirements, healthcare workflows, and technical implementation. I translate
        nurse workflows into user stories, alert logic, and usability criteria for a Telegram-based
        AI prototype designed to support patient-deterioration monitoring. I also coordinate weekly
        agendas, presentations, and recap emails across client, partner, and UCSD stakeholders to
        align technical decisions with end-user needs and keep the project moving toward implementation.
      </div>
      <div class="tl-badges">
        <span class="tl-badge">GenAI</span>
        <span class="tl-badge">SQL</span>
        <span class="tl-badge">Python</span>
        <span class="tl-badge">AI Tools</span>
      </div>
    </div>

    <div class="tl-item">
      <div class="tl-when">Apr 2023 — Jun 2025</div>
      <div class="tl-title">Data Analyst</div>
      <div class="tl-org">Morningstar DBRS — Chicago, IL</div>
      <div class="tl-desc">
        Improved reporting workflows, data quality, and stakeholder visibility across structured
        finance teams. I standardized 20+ Tableau Prep workflows and dashboards by translating
        stakeholder reporting needs into reusable metrics, filters, and documentation — giving
        10+ teams self-service access to deal-inflow and issuance insights. I also validated SQL,
        Python, and Excel reporting workflows by reconciling source data to dashboard outputs,
        debugging metric inconsistencies, and improving trust in internal reporting.
      </div>
      <div class="tl-badges">
        <span class="tl-badge">SQL</span>
        <span class="tl-badge">Python</span>
        <span class="tl-badge">Tableau</span>
      </div>
    </div>

    <div class="tl-item">
      <div class="tl-when">Jul 2022 — Apr 2023</div>
      <div class="tl-title">Credit Ratings Analyst</div>
      <div class="tl-org">Morningstar DBRS — Chicago, IL</div>
      <div class="tl-desc">
        Supported credit decision-making through quantitative financial analysis, scenario modeling,
        and disciplined quality checks. I performed 15+ cash-flow and scenario analyses across
        multiple commercial real estate property types under tight deadlines and high-volume deal
        flow, maintaining 99% accuracy across complex financial models through structured QA reviews
        and consistent validation of assumptions and outputs.
      </div>
      <div class="tl-badges">
        <span class="tl-badge">Excel</span>
        <span class="tl-badge">SQL</span>
      </div>
    </div>

    <div class="tl-item">
      <div class="tl-when">Jun 2021 — Jul 2021</div>
      <div class="tl-title">Machine Learning Intern</div>
      <div class="tl-org">Centre for Development of Advanced Computing (CDAC)</div>
      <div class="tl-desc">
        Supported an image-distortion recognition project focused on incremental learning and
        similarity-based image comparison. I developed a beta incremental-learning model using
        NumPy, Pandas, TensorFlow, and Keras to support continuous model updates as new image data
        was introduced, achieving 0.981 testing and 0.999 training accuracy. I also implemented a
        similarity-index approach to compare original and distorted images.
      </div>
      <div class="tl-badges">
        <span class="tl-badge">Python</span>
        <span class="tl-badge">TensorFlow</span>
        <span class="tl-badge">Keras</span>
      </div>
    </div>
  </div>
</div>
</main>

<footer class="site-footer">
  <div class="footer-inner">
    <span>Based in San Diego, CA · UCSD MS Business Analytics</span>
    <div class="footer-links">
      <a href="https://linkedin.com/in/eesha-jagdhane" target="_blank" rel="noopener">LinkedIn</a>
      <a href="https://github.com/eeshajagdhane?tab=repositories" target="_blank" rel="noopener">GitHub</a>
      <a href="mailto:ejagdhane99@gmail.com">Email</a>
      <a href="resume.pdf" target="_blank" rel="noopener">Resume</a>
    </div>
    <span>© 2026 Eesha Jagdhane</span>
  </div>
</footer>

<script>
  const io = new IntersectionObserver((entries) => {
    entries.forEach(e => { if (e.isIntersecting) e.target.classList.add('visible'); });
  }, { threshold: 0.08 });
  document.querySelectorAll('.reveal').forEach(el => io.observe(el));
</script>
```
